rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Requests For Admission To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Requests For Admission To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Pro-Tem Judge Ernst Halperin discloses that twenty years ago during the summer of 1992 he was employed as a summer associate with the firm of Natkin Weisbach Higginbotham. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under California Code of Judicial Ethics Canon 6D(3)(a)(vi), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. The motion to compel is GRANTED with respect to Requests for Admission 1, 2, 3, 5, 16, 18, 19, 21, 21.5, 22, 23, 44, 45, and 49 (as written with the January 1, 2009 date stated in RFA 49). DENIED in all other respects. Request for sanctions is DENIED. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Sepcial Interrogatories To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Sepcial Interrogatories To Deft Lisa Leebove And Steve Steinberg And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pro-Tem Judge Ernst Halperin discloses that twenty years ago during the summer of 1992 he was employed as a summer associate with the firm of Natkin Weisbach Higginbotham. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under California Code of Judicial Ethics 6D(3)(a)(vi), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. The motion to compel is GRANTED with respect to Specially Prepared Interrogatories 1 through 46 and 62 through 78. DENIED with respect to Specially Prepared Interrogatories 47 through 51, 57 through 61 & 79. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS, INC.'S Motion To Seal. The motion is granted because it is unopposed and meets the requirements of CRC 2.550. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the limitation of liability clause of section 13 of the parties' agreement applies to this case or is excepted. The exception is ambiguous and parol evidence is necessary to resolve the dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: YUSEF JOHNS et al VS. S.F. TOWN TAXI INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion Compelling Deft Mohinder Singh To Appear For His Deposition An Req For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF YUSEF JOHNS Ntc Of Motion Compelling Deft Mohinder Singh To Appear For His Deposition An Req For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to submit three possible dates for the deposition that are mutually agreeable and within the next three weeks. The declaration of Karish Rajadhyksha states that he made numerous telephone calls to Mr. Singh during September and October regarding his availability for deposition, and Mr. Singh did not return the calls. It would also be helpful to the court for counsel for Defendants to be prepared to address what efforts have been made since November 2nd to reach Mr. Singh and whether he has responded to those efforts. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO LOPEZ et al VS. MICHAEL BERGMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Terminating Sanctions Should Not Be Granted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 8, Order To Show Cause Why Terminating Sanctions Should Not Be Granted Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin , a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No response to the Order to Show Cause having been submitted, the terminating sanctions requested by Defendant are GRANTED pursuant to CCP Section 2023.030(d)(3). Counsel for Defendant is to prepare an order. The order shall describe in a summary fashion the discovery propounded, the motion to compel, plaintiff's failure to oppose the motion to compel, the court's order dated August 29, 2012, Defendant's further motion to compel and request for terminating sanctions, plaintiff's failure to respond to the motion, the Court's November 9th order setting an order to show cause for December 11th and plaintiff's failure to respond to the order to show cause. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., GOOGLE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. Certain portions of the contract support FCC's assertion that it is an intended third party beneficiary and the Court cannot conclude as a matter of law that it was merely an incidental beneficiary of the contract at this point of the litigation. The Court concludes that plaintiffs allege wrongful and unfair conduct to support the tort and UCL causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PARALLAX VOLATILITY ADVISERS LLC VS. VIXSTAR CAPITAL LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012, Line 28, Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction. Granted. None of the defendants oppose the issuance of preliminary injunction, except to clarify certain terms. The preliminary injunction is issued with the following modifications. Nothing in this order shall prevent the parties from accessing or using documents or files for purposes in this litigation only, subject to a protective order. Nothing in this order shall prohibit Defendants from using publicly available data or information, including but not limited to historical and current market data, for any legitimate purposes, on the condition such information is not accessed, viewed, used, or taken directly from any data, documents or property taken from or belonging to Parallax Volatility Advisers, LLC. To the extent that Parallax Volatility Advisers, LLC requests a protective order, the parties may file a stipulation and proposed protective order by ex parte application. If the parties cannot agree on the language of the protective order, the parties may file a noticed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)
